text:
Smart creators are quietly building football UTube channels right now.

And they are getting millions of views doing it.

Here is exactly how to build yours from scratch using only free tools 👇

Why football channels are exploding right now
The FIFA World Cup just ended.

Hundreds of millions of people are still searching for match analysis, player stories, behind the scenes content and football history.

The search volume is enormous.

The competition in the documentary and storytelling lane is still low.

This window will not stay open forever.

Step 1 — Pick your football channel angle
Do not just create a general football channel.
Pick one specific lane.

These are the ones getting the most views right now:
🏆 Untold stories of legendary players
🏆 Match breakdowns and tactical analysis
🏆 Rise and fall stories of football clubs
🏆 World Cup historical moments
🏆 Forgotten players who should have been legends
🏆 Behind the scenes of football transfers

Pick one lane. Stay in it. Own it.

Step 2 — Find your content using Google
Go to Google.

Search your chosen angle.

Example: "untold story of [player name]" or "World Cup 2026 controversies"

Find three to five well written articles from trusted sports sites like ESPN, BBC Sport, Sky Sports or FIFA. com.

Copy the URLs of the best ones.

Step 3 — Take everything to NotebookLM
Go to notebooklm. google. com

It is completely free.

Click New Notebook.

Paste all your article URLs as sources.

Then ask it:
"Analyse all these sources and give me a full documentary style script outline. Include an engaging opening hook, three main sections with key facts and a powerful closing that leaves the viewer wanting more."

NotebookLM reads every article and builds you a complete blueprint instantly.

No writing from scratch.
No hours of research.

Just a structured outline ready to turn into a script.

Step 4 — Write your full script with Claude AI
Copy the outline NotebookLM gave you.

Open Claude AI.

Paste it and type:
"Turn this outline into a full 8 minute documentary style YouTube script. Use a storytelling tone like a Netflix documentary narrator. Start with a hook that makes someone stop scrolling in the first 20 seconds. Keep the language engaging and simple."

Your full professional script is ready in minutes.

Step 5 — Create your voiceover
Open ElevenLabs free plan.
Paste your script.
Choose a deep authoritative voice that matches documentary energy.

Download your voiceover completely free.

Step 6 — Find your visuals
Go to these completely free sources:
🎥 Pexels — Free football stadium and crowd footage
🎥 Pixabay — Free sports and action footage
🖼️ Unsplash — Free high quality football images for thumbnails

For match clips and player footage use officially licensed clips from YouTube's free creative commons library.

Search: "[player name] creative commons" on YouTube.

Step 7 — Edit your documentary video
Open CapCut desktop.
Import your voiceover.
Layer your visuals over the audio.
Match the visuals to what the narrator is describing.
Add automatic captions.
Add dramatic background music from YouTube Audio Library.
Search: "epic cinematic" or "dramatic sports" in the library.
Export completely free. No watermark.

Step 8 — Design your thumbnail
- Open Canva.
- Search: "YouTube documentary thumbnail template"
- Pick a dark cinematic template.
- Add the player or team name in bold text.
- Add one powerful image.

Keep it clean. Keep it dramatic.

Study the thumbnails of channels like GOAL, Football Daily and These Football Times for inspiration.

Step 9 — Set up your channel correctly

- Create your YouTube channel.
- Name it something specific to your lane.
Example: "Football Untold" or "The Football Files" or "World Cup Stories"
- Write a channel description that tells YouTube exactly - what your channel is about.
- Use keywords like: football documentary, football history, World Cup stories, player stories.
- Upload a clean professional banner using Canva free templates.

Step 10 — Upload and optimize
- Upload your video.
- Open VidIQ free plan.
-Research the best keywords for your specific topic.
Example for a Ronaldo story: "Ronaldo untold story" "Ronaldo early life" "Ronaldo documentary"
- Add these keywords naturally to your title, description and tags.
- Post your video.

The first 48 hours of views tell YouTube whether to recommend your video or bury it.

Tools used:
🔍 Google — Find source articles free
📓 NotebookLM — Research and outline free
✍️ Claude AI — Full script writing free
🎙️ ElevenLabs — Professional voiceover free
🎬 CapCut — Video editing free
🎥 Pexels and Pixabay — Stock footage free
🖼️ Canva — Thumbnail design free
📊 VidIQ — SEO and optimisation free

Total cost: $0
Total time for first video: 3 to 4 hours

The World Cup audience is still searching.

The storytelling lane is still open.

The tools are all free.

The only thing missing is your decision to start.

If you want me to walk you through every single step of this live.

From picking your niche to uploading your first video.

Batch B of my 3 day live training is ongoing.

Everything is covered from scratch to finish.

Link is in my bio. 🎯

$PLTR --- $PLTR management aggressively raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance from the initial $7.2 billion to $7.65–$7.662 billion — implying 71% year-over-year growth — and raised the floor for U.S. commercial revenue growth to 120%.

Google Cloud Marketplace launch: Delivered bi-directional integration between BigQuery and Palantir Foundry, plus deep connectivity between Google Gemini and Palantir AIP.

Cross-industry mega-partnerships: Teamed up with elite law firm Kirkland & Ellis to build an AI-powered private equity fundraising platform; struck a strategic partnership with construction giant McCarthy to bring AI to construction and engineering.

SAP data migration integration: In May, partnered with SAP to launch AI-powered data migration tools, directly targeting the most painful pain point for global enterprises: ERP cloud transformation.

1. The AIP Bootcamp Business Model That Is Crushing Enterprise Sales
Traditional enterprise software sales cycles typically stretch 6–12 months. Palantir's AIP Bootcamps let customers bring their engineers and actual business pain points to build production-ready AI agents and workflows on Palantir's platform in just 1–5 days. This "try-before-you-buy" paradigm that delivers immediate, tangible value helped the company close 206 seven-figure+ deals in Q1 alone (including 47 eight-figure deals), driving exponential customer base expansion.

2. The Ultimate Rule of 40 Outlier
In enterprise software, a Rule of 40 score (revenue growth rate + profit margin) above 40% is considered elite. Palantir posted a staggering 145% Rule of 40 score in Q1. As CEO Alex Karp noted, the only companies in global tech hitting these kinds of metrics are hardcore compute hardware giants like Nvidia, Micron, and SK Hynix — Palantir is the only software company in the world at this level.

3. Ontology Is Its Unreplicable Moat
Large language models have no inherent logic. For enterprises, the hardest part of adopting AI isn't accessing models — it's organizing messy, siloed finance, supply chain, and HR data. Palantir's core Ontology technology transforms unstructured data into virtual models that map directly to real-world business operations, letting its AIP seamlessly integrate any state-of-the-art LLM with near-zero hallucinations and enterprise-grade compliance and security. This underlying architecture is at least 5 years ahead of peers.

Stop paying for separate tools to create product photos, virtual try ons, and video ads.

@itsPolloAI just launched Pollo AI Commerce Studio, and it combines all three inside a single workspace.

Most e commerce workflows still look like this:

Upload product image
Generate product photos in one tool 
Create try on images in another 
Build video ads somewhere else 
Repeat uploads every time

Pollo AI Commerce Studio takes a different approach.

Upload your product once and generate:

AI Product Shots
Virtual Try On Photos
Product Showcase Videos

all from the same workbench.

What stood out to me is that it's designed for sellers, not designers.

No prompt engineering.
No switching between apps.
No complicated editing timelines.

You can choose from 100+ templates across categories like apparel, beauty, electronics, food, and home goods, then generate assets with just a few clicks.

For small sellers, dropshippers, Amazon, Etsy, Shopify, and TikTok Shop operators, reducing workflow friction can be just as important as improving the final creative.

Pollo AI Commerce Studio feels like a step toward making e-commerce content creation faster, simpler, and more accessible.
